data.title: Update project docs to reflect large-refactor review path
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update CLAUDE.md's architecture section and quick reference to describe the new large-commit review path. Update any other docs that reference the 600-line hard rejection or review routing behavior that is now stale. Add a supersession note to large-diff-splitting-guide.md if applicable.

**Why**: CLAUDE.md's tiered review section currently states "300+ lines → opus upgrade; 600+ → rejection." After this epic ships, the 600-line rejection is replaced by SIZE_WARNING (non-blocking), and commits with >=20 files are routed through REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md. Without this update, future agents reading CLAUDE.md will attempt to split commits to avoid a rejection that can no longer occur, or fail to recognize the SIZE_WARNING signal.

**Scope**:
- IN: CLAUDE.md architecture section update (tiered review description, SIZE_WARNING behavior, huge diff review path), CLAUDE.md quick reference update if new commands or config keys are added, large-diff-splitting-guide.md supersession note if warranted by changed behavior
- OUT: new documentation files, implementation details, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md (Story S6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md's tiered review description reflects the new behavior: 300+ lines → opus upgrade, 600+ lines → SIZE_WARNING (non-blocking), >=20 files → routed to REVIEW-WORKFLOW-HUGE.md
  ← Satisfies: All epic success criteria (documentation ensures future agents operate correctly within the new system)
- When this story is complete, no other existing documentation file in the repository describes the 600-line hard rejection as current behavior without a supersession note or correction

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
